inputs.value: A high-quality studio photograph of a simple and neat, with exactly 10 to 30 glass pieces, physical handcrafted stained glass suncatcher representing a whimsical wizard gnome, isolated on a plain crisp solid white background. The gnome is depicted in a symmetric full body view, featuring a tall, curved pointed purple hat with a brim, a large rounded white beard, and a wide flowing cloak in alternating stripes of lavender and white. Constructed from gorgeous pieces of textured translucent colored glass. All glass segments are bound by thick, continuous, smooth dark solder copper foil joints that completely partition the design into closed cells representing real pieces of glass, with absolutely no loose lines, no hanging lines, and no impossible narrow cuts. To ensure physical feasibility, a structural solder seam must connect the brim of the hat to the top of the beard, and vertical solder lines divide the cloak into five distinct, smooth long panels. Designed for realistic physical glass cutting and assembly, with extremely smooth easy-to-cut outlines, no narrow shapes, and no complex jigsaw lines. Realistic glass fabrication template with no impossible sharp inner angles or tiny pieces.
